subject: ENGLISH
title: Clap the Syllables
objective: Count spoken syllables in familiar words.
introduction: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Clap the Syllables”.
A key word used in the lesson “Clap the Syllables”.
A key word used in the lesson “Clap the Syllables”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Assuming every vowel letter is a separate syllable

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: How many spoken beats are in banana?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. one and five do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Clap the Syllables”?

options.text: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.
Assuming every vowel letter is a separate syllable

items.explanation: The correct response is “A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.

options.text: Assuming every vowel letter is a separate syllable

items.explanation: The correct response is “ta-ble has two beats” because it follows the lesson explanation: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Counting letters instead of beats” because it follows the lesson explanation: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.

items.prompt: How many spoken beats are in banana?
items.explanation: The correct response is “three” because it follows the lesson explanation: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “ba-na-na has three beats” because it follows the lesson explanation: A syllable is a spoken beat with a vowel sound. Clapping helps hear beats but does not by itself determine spelling.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Clap the Syllables; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Clap the Syllables.
